title: tests: Replace ncat for socat
body:
Drop last usage of ncat in tests for socat.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: This patch works in podman 5.5.2 on Tumbleweed: https://openqa.opensuse.org/tests/5274064 (unrelated failure on `podman run --blkio-weight-device`).
i have weak bash-fu but this LGTM

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99*, *ricardobranco777* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> /lgtm
> 
> Thanks, I think that means we are ncat free everywhere now?

Yes.  Only `nc` remaining but it's safe.

> Yes. Only nc remaining but it's safe.

right and only in testimage (I think?!) which should be the static version not effected by the host distro changes so that should not cause unexpected new problems

> > Yes. Only nc remaining but it's safe.
> 
> right and only in testimage (I think?!) which should be the static version not effected by the host distro changes so that should not cause unexpected new problems

We use nc also in helpers for `tcp_port_probe()` which could be modified to use Bash's `/dev/tcp/$host/$port`.  We're also doing stuff like `echo "$teststring" | nc 127.0.0.1 $port_out` in `200-pod` that can also use Bash for this.

Will submit another PR to get rid of netcat for good in the host.
Ah, thanks. As we depend on socat anyway it should be fine to use socat there as well if that is easier.

> Ah, thanks. As we depend on socat anyway it should be fine to use socat there as well if that is easier.

With Bash we don't spawn new processes.  Please check out https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26921 so I create a VR in openQA.
